violations.text: The floors in the dry storge area has an accumulation rodent droppings. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 04/01/2026

The following was observed: 1) The sides of the walk-in cooler were observed with a gap in between the walls 2) An outlet next to the desk was observed not in use 3) The pipe leading into the wall and electrical panel near the desk was observed with a gap These areas are possible entry points for rodents and must be sealed. New Violation. Correct By: 04/15/2026

Omlette sandwiches were observed held at 114 degrees Fahrenheit. Per PIC, foods were to be disposed of due to breakfast ending. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be maintained at 135 degrees Fahrenheit or above except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/16/2026

Cheese sticks were observed held at 45 degrees Fahrenheit at the cold rail. Foods moved into the walk-in cooler to rapidly cool. Per PIC, foods were placed in the cold rail 2 hours prior to inspection. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. New Violation. Correct By: 03/16/2026

The floors under the dishwasher and in the dry storage area has an accumulation of rodent droppings. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 03/16/2026
The floors in the dry storage area were observed with an accumulation of rodent droppings. New Violation. Correct By: 10/01/2025

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Sandwiches and salads were placed into plastic containers with lids and stacked on top of each other. Food observed at 60 degrees Fahrenheit in the reach-in refrigerator. Foods prepped under an hour prior to inspection. Plastic containers opened and foods placed onto sheet trays and moved into walk-in freezer to rapidly cool. Cooling methods reviewed with staff at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/12/2025

The floors in the corners of the dry storage area observed with old droppings. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 12/10/2024
The Person in charge did not assure compliance with critical code requirements. New Violation. Correct By: 12/03/2024

Observed several adulterated granolas(cereal) with rodent gnaw marks and droppings on several canned goods. PIC voluntary discarded adulterated items during this inspection. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 12/03/2024

The presence of pest is not controlled as evidenced by rodent droppings observed on hot/cold holding equipment, canned goods, and the floors in the dry storage area. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. -Staff voluntarily disposed of the food products during this inspection. -Pest control service by a licensed professional must conduct a full assessment/evaluation of kitchen facility. -Pest control report must be provided to inspector upon the next re-inspection date. -A plan of action of how to eliminate the problem must be provided to inspector. New Violation. Correct By: 12/03/2024

The non-food contact surfaces of the kitchen vent have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. The non-food contact surfaces shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep it clean. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 12/03/2024

Observed the floors in the dry storage area and observed the non-food contact surfaces on the shelving underneath the hot/cold holding equipment with an accumulation of rodent droppings. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 12/03/2024
A can of pickles was observed with rodent droppings. Person in charge voluntarily disposed of food at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/17/2024

The non-food contact surfaces of the hot/cold-holding equipment on the line has an accumulation of dust and old rodent droppings. Equipment was cleaned and sanitized at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/17/2024

The floors in the dry storage area has an accumulation of old rodent droppings. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 10/17/2024

Unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment or items were observed in the dry storage area. Establishments shall be kept free of unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment and litter to prevent harborage conditions. New Violation. Correct By: 10/17/2024

The floor in the back dry storage area has an accumulation of old rodent droppings. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 03/06/2024

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety with RIDOH Center for Food Protection at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. New Violation. Correct By: 03/20/2024
A can of olives was observed to be in contact with rodent droppings. Person in charge disposed of food at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/06/2024

The floor in the back dry storage area has an accumulation of old rodent droppings. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 03/06/2024

At the time of inspection, the 2 Door Reach-in cooler was observed to be out of service (unplugged) and awaiting repair/service. Unit is to be serviced/repaired or removed if not to be fixed. Per the PIC, another work order was placed for repair prior to re-inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 10/02/2023

At the time of inspection, the handwashing sink faucet along the back wall prep area near the deli slicers was observed to be in poor repair. The cold water handle to the faucet was observed to be "stripped"/Worn and incapable of turning on the cold water. The faucet is to be repaired. A HANDWASHING SINK shall be maintained so that it is accessible at all times for EMPLOYEE use. New Violation. Correct By: 10/11/2023

At the time of inspection, the 2 Door Reach-In Cooler located in the kitchen area was observed to be not in use (Operating at an ambient temperature of 94 degrees Fahrenheit). Per the PIC, a work order was set up with East Greenwich High School for the repair to the cold holing unit. Unit is to be repaired/replaced or removed if no longer to be used. New Violation. Correct By: 10/11/2023

The floor in the walk-in freezer storage area was observed to have an accumulation of soil residue and old rodent droppings behind and under equipment. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean and to aid in the monitoring of the pest control program. New Violation. Correct By: 09/27/2023
